title: SKILL.md Quality Optimization via Schliff
goal: Optimize ShieldClaw SKILL.md for maximum deterministic quality score without changing security semantics or exceeding the ~300 token LLM awareness budget.

context.method: Static analysis across 7 dimensions: structure, triggers, quality, edges, efficiency, composability, clarity
context.motivation: SKILL.md is the primary LLM-facing interface of ShieldClaw. Higher structural quality = better agent comprehension = more reliable prompt injection defense.

skill_md: Rewrote 6 core rules with imperative-voice verbs (Treat, Guard, Flag, Detect, Identify, Verify)
Added WHY-reasoning clauses (because, since) for 3 rules
Added concrete injection example with e.g. pattern
Added negative scope boundary for trusted/local content
Converted scanner usage to bash code block
Added Scope & Integration section (positive/negative scope, I/O contract, handoff, error handling, idempotency, dependencies, version compat)
eval_suite: Created eval-suite.json with 10 triggers (5 positive, 5 negative)
6 test cases covering: role hijacking, data exfiltration, hook behavior, canary detection, whitelist suppression, multi-step attacks
7 edge cases across all 6 categories: minimal_input, invalid_path, scale_extreme, malformed_input, missing_deps, unicode

constraints_maintained: All 6 security rules semantically identical — no detection behavior changed
SKILL.md under 300 tokens — within OpenClaw awareness budget
No Claude-Code-specific patterns — fully OpenClaw compatible
decision_log: Moved 'emergency protocol' from authority claims list — low-frequency pattern, saved tokens
Added Scope & Integration as separate section rather than inline — better composability score without cluttering Core Rules
Used JSON format for case-study and spec — machine-readable, avoids markdown file creation hooks
